open_questions: Cumulative discount behavior is undefined in openapi/orders.yaml: the implementation recalculates total from the base price (100) on every POST, not from the current discounted total â€” is this the intended contract behavior for stacked discounts?

openapi/orders.yaml contains no response schemas (fields, types) for either endpoint; field-level assertions in the new tests (id, total, discounts) are inferred from server.js implementation rather than a documented contract â€” should response schemas be added to the OpenAPI spec?

e2e-ui-tests-1 uses shared test_env with no access to orders-api directly; the cart total consumer integration test (PR-orders-api-201/ui-cart-total-consumer-integration) is marked todo until the UI test repo gains compose mode or a coordinated integration environment.

contract.rationale: Contract file openapi/orders.yaml changed alongside implementation. Existing test coverage for orders-api endpoints exists (PROJ-88, PROJ-61) and must be validated against the updated contract. Resolution engine detected cross-repo impact on web-storefront-ui consumer with confidence 1.0 â€” test verification required in e2e-ui-tests-1. No new uncovered behavior areas identified; prefer updating existing mapped tests over creating new ones.
